<question>
1. a is laurel.
2. a is south.
3. If a is north and a is laurel, then b is cobalt.
4. If b is cobalt, then b is north.
5. If a is south and a is laurel, then b is coral.
6. If b is coral, then b is north.
7. If a is west and a is laurel, then b is ivory.
8. If b is ivory, then b is north.
9. If a is east and a is laurel, then b is lime.
10. If b is lime, then b is north.
Which state applies to b?
</question>

<formal>
<constants>
a = a
b = b
</constants>
<predicates>
Ax: x is coral
Bx: x is cobalt
Cx: x is ivory
Dx: x is laurel
Ex: x is lime
Fx: x is north
Gx: x is south
Hx: x is east
Ix: x is west
</predicates>
<premises>
Da
Ga
Fa & Da -> Bb
Bb -> Fb
Ga & Da -> Ab
Ab -> Fb
Ia & Da -> Cb
Cb -> Fb
Ha & Da -> Eb
Eb -> Fb
</premises>
<proof>
Da ; R
Ga ; R
Ab ; ->E
Fb ; ->E
</proof>
<conclusion>
Fb
</conclusion>
</formal>
<answer>
coral
</answer>
